meta.tagline: Tracking every Anthropic & Claude launch — and how it stacks up against the field.

resources.intro: Every Anthropic resource worth following — straight from primary sources, organized so nothing slips past your feed.

latest.name: Claude Opus 4.8
latest.headline: Anthropic's most capable model yet — better coding, reasoning, and honesty.
highlights: Agentic coding score up from 64.3% to 69.2%
~4× less likely to let a code flaw pass without comment
Fast mode runs at 2.5× speed and 3× cheaper than before
Dynamic workflows: hundreds of parallel subagents in Claude Code

timeline.title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
timeline.summary: The US government has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national, whether inside or outside the United States.

timeline.title: Results from first Anthropic Public Record
timeline.summary: Anthropic Public Record is a national survey of attitudes and opinions towards AI.

timeline.title: TCS and Anthropic partner to bring Claude to regulated industries
timeline.summary: We’re announcing a partnership with Tata Consultancy Services (TCS). TCS will provide Claude to 50,000 of its own employees across 56 countries; build Claude-powered products for clients in financial services, healthcare, the public sector, and other regulated industries; and join the Claude Partner Network.

timeline.title: DXC will integrate Claude into the systems banks, airlines, and other regulated industries rely on
timeline.summary: We’re announcing a multi-year global alliance with DXC Technology, one of the world’s largest IT services companies.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Corps
timeline.summary: We’re launching Claude Corps, a national fellowship program for people early in their careers who are passionate about extending the benefits of AI to communities across America.

timeline.title: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
timeline.summary: Today we’re launching Claude Fable 5: a Mythos-class model that we’ve made safe for general use.

timeline.title: Anthropic co-founder Chris Olah's remarks on Pope Leo XIV's encyclical "Magnifica humanitas"
timeline.summary: The full text of Chris Olah's remarks on the Pope's encyclical on AI.

timeline.title: Widening the conversation on frontier AI
timeline.summary: Anthropic is engaging scholars, clergy, and philosophers from diverse traditions to shape Claude’s moral character and ensure powerful AI acts for the global good.

timeline.title: Introducing the Services Track and Partner Hub of the Claude Partner Network
timeline.summary: In March, we launched the Claude Partner Network, a program for the firms that help enterprises put Claude into production.. Today, we’re announcing two new components that make this ecosystem easier for customers to navigate.

timeline.title: Expanding Project Glasswing
timeline.summary: We’re extending Project Glasswing to approximately 150 new organizations in more than fifteen countries

timeline.title: Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC
timeline.summary: Anthropic has confidentially submitted a draft S-1 registration statement to the Securities and Exchange Commission

timeline.title: Anthropic raises $65B in Series H funding at $965B post-money valuation
timeline.summary: Anthropic has raised $65 billion in Series H funding led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs
timeline.summary: Today, we’re launching Claude Design, a new Anthropic Labs product that lets you collaborate with Claude to create polished visual work like designs, prototypes, slides, one-pagers, and more.

timeline.title: Anthropic opens Milan office to support Italian enterprise, research, and developers
timeline.summary: We're opening a new office in Milan, our sixth in Europe.

timeline.title: Claude Opus 4.8
timeline.summary: New flagship. Gains across coding, agentic skills, reasoning and knowledge work. Adds Dynamic workflows (parallel subagents), Effort control, and mid-task system messages in the API.

timeline.title: Anthropic appoints KiYoung Choi as Representative Director of Korea
timeline.summary: KiYoung Choi is joining Anthropic as Representative Director of Korea, ahead of the opening of our Seoul office.

timeline.title: KPMG integrates Claude across its core business and workforce of more than 276,000 in strategic alliance
timeline.summary: KPMG will deploy Claude across its 276,000+ global employees and embed it into its Digital Gateway platform to enhance client services in tax, legal, and private equity work.

timeline.title: Anthropic acquires Stainless
timeline.summary: Anthropic acquired Stainless, a company specializing in SDK generation and MCP server tooling, to help Claude agents connect more easily with external data and systems.

timeline.title: PwC is deploying Claude to build technology, execute deals, and reinvent enterprise functions for clients
timeline.summary: PwC is deploying Claude across its workforce of hundreds of thousands of professionals to build software, execute deals, and transform enterprise functions like finance, HR, and supply chain for clients.

timeline.title: Anthropic forms $200 million partnership with the Gates Foundation
timeline.summary: Anthropic and the Gates Foundation are co-investing $200 million to deploy Claude across global health, education, and economic mobility programs in low- and middle-income countries over four years.

upcoming.title: Claude Opus 4.9 / 5.0
upcoming.summary: Expected next flagship step. Watch for larger effort-control range and deeper agent orchestration.

upcoming.title: Expanded dynamic workflows
upcoming.summary: Parallel subagent orchestration expanding beyond Enterprise/Team/Max in Claude Code.

upcoming.title: Longer context tier
upcoming.summary: Competitive pressure from 2M-token rivals; a larger context option is plausible.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.8
models.note: Best-in-class agentic coding and the most reliable at flagging its own mistakes. The reference point for this comparison.

models.name: GPT-5.5
models.note: Strongest all-rounder; broad ecosystem and tooling. Slightly pricier on input than Claude.

models.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.note: Leads published reasoning benchmarks and is the fastest of the group. Cheapest output among the frontier tier.

models.name: Grok 4
models.note: Largest context window at 2M tokens; strong raw coding scores.

models.name: DeepSeek V4-Pro
models.note: By far the cheapest frontier option — open-weight MoE, ~1.6T params. Great cost-to-performance.

models.name: Llama 4 Maverick
models.note: Open weights, self-hostable. Lower benchmark scores but unbeatable on price and control.

models.name: Mistral Large 3
models.note: European, strong on efficiency and multilingual tasks. Smaller context window than rivals.

samples.text: Opus 4.8 is genuinely the best coding model I've used — caught a race condition Cursor missed.
samples.label: positive

samples.text: Love that 4.8 flags its own uncertainty now instead of bluffing. Huge for trust.
samples.label: positive

samples.text: Fast mode on 4.8 still feels slow for me and the pricing stings vs Gemini.
samples.label: negative

samples.text: Solid bump but honestly nothing mind-blowing over 4.7 for my workflow.
samples.label: neutral

benchmarks.note: SWE-bench Pro (coding) and a blended reasoning index (GPQA/ARC-AGI). Figures are approximate, sourced from public leaderboards; see Sources. Lower is better for price.

metrics.label: Reasoning index
metrics.desc: Blended GPQA Diamond / ARC-AGI style reasoning score.

metrics.label: Output speed
metrics.desc: Generation throughput in tokens per second.

metrics.label: Output price
metrics.desc: Cost per million output tokens (lower is better).
